title: elaeocarpus subserratus
intro: Elaeocarpus subserratus, also known as the Blue Olive Berry or Blue Olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to Australia. The leaves and berries of this plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Elaeocarpus subserratus are said to help with various health benefits, including wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
